free_text: Photoneutron Cross Sections for Aluminum and Silicon
to 62 MeV
Iowa State University 70-MeV
synchrotron.
The collimated bremsstrahlung beam.
The samples were cylinders of at least 99.7% purity
and of 5 cm diam.
While accumulating data for a single yield
point the detection equipment and the synchrotron
were run through 50 irradiation and counting cycles.
After a bombardment, a 50-msec delay time elapsed.
BP
OS. REACTION coding in subs.3,5 changed
OS. Isomer code added in sub.4
Factor of 0.9
was introduced during the data analysis.
Least Structure analysis.
Horizontal bars (given as EN-RSL) indicate the
resolution function width.
Vertical bars indicate errors in cross
section.
Contributions due to other multiparticle reactions
leading to beta+ emitters of several seconds are not
excluded, e.g. Al(g,3n).
Fig. 4, page 980. Combination of discrete
points and continuous curves, and the points for
digitization were determined by the compiler.
FCT=2 due to detection of annihilation events twice
(26Si -> 26mAl -> 26gAl)
Horizontal bars (given as EN-RSL) indicate the
resolution function width.
Vertical bars indicate errors in cross
section.
Contributions due to other multiparticle reactions
leading to beta+ emitters of several seconds are not
excluded, e.g. Si-28(g,nd) and small contributions by
multineutron reactions on Si-29, 30.
Fig. 5, page 980. Combination of discrete
points and continuous curves, and the points for
digitization were determined by the compiler.

introduced during the data analysis.

Absolute values were determined using data by
Thompson et al., Ferrero et al., and Haslam et al.
